name: paragradio

metadata.name: paragradio
metadata.summary: Parallel GNU Radio. Launches a flowgraph using `multiprocessing`, provides methods for flowgraph. Includes pre-built flowgraphs.

documentation:
# paragradio
    
paragradio (Parallel GNU Radio) module.
    
Note that much of the functionality also depends on GNU Radio. However, `gnuradio` is not listed in the dependencies as it is not pip-installable (as far as the authors know).

Also uses PyQt5, which you likely have if you have gnuradio.
    
### Example usage
    
```python3
from paragradio.v2025_01 import SpecAnSim

sa = SpecAnSim()
sa.start()
```
   
### Version / breaking changes policy

Versions are marked by year and month. We will adhere to the following policy:
    
- Before the version's month and year, that version is unstable, and changes will be made freely.
- Once that version's month and year arrive, that version is stable, and only non-breaking changes will be made.

Examples:

| Version  | Unstable              | Stable                 |
|----------|-----------------------|------------------------|
| v2025_02 | Before Feb 1, 2025    | Starting Feb 1, 2025   | 
| v2025_05 | Before May 1, 2025    | Starting May 1, 2025   | 
| v2026_01 | Before Jan 1, 2026    | Starting Jan 1, 2026   | 
| etc.     |                       |                        |
    
### Current status

As of January 2025, we are using this to teach a class. We are still deciding what the roadmap will be. Pull requests are welcome.

### License

Same license as GNU Radio.

functions.name: paragradio.v2025_02._internal.core._grc_main_prep
documentation:
This is a copy/paste of the main() function that is generated
by GRC for Graphical (Qt) flowgraphs. It omits the last line
of that main function, `qapp.exec_()`, because it is blocking.
The developer using this function is responsible for running `.exec_()`.
Example usage 1:
```python
tb, qapp = _grc_main_prep(a_specific_gr_top_block)
qapp.exec_()
```
Example usage 2:
```python
tb, qapp = _grc_main_prep(a_specific_gr_top_block)
## hypothetically, if your `tb` has a signal source block
tb.signal_source.set_frequency(101.3e6)
qapp.exec_()
```

functions.name: paragradio.v2025_02._internal.core._processcmds
documentation:
Pulls a command from the queue `q`, and calls it
with `instance` as the first argument. `instance` is often a `gr.top_block`
(which is conventionally named `tb` in the .py files generated by GRC.)

functions.name: paragradio.v2025_02._internal.core._event_loop_gr
documentation:
Run the Qt/GR process.
- Sets up the top_block similarly to the `main()` function in the .py file that GRC generates
- Starts a repeating Qt timer to get and execute commands from `q`
- Runs the Qt application (assumes that `top_block_cls` is a Qt-based GR flowgraph)

variables.name: paragradio.v2025_02._internal.core.Tgr
documentation: A gr top block or a subclass of such.

variables.name: paragradio.v2025_02._internal.core.TPFunc
documentation:
A function which takes at least one argument.
In this particular project, the first argument is most often 
an instance of `gr.top_block` or any subclass.

Examples:

```python
def f(tb) -> None: ...
def f(tb, a) -> None: ...
def f(tb, a, b) -> None: ...
def f(tb, a, b, c) -> None: ...
# etc...
```

variables.name: paragradio.v2025_02._internal.core._cmdqueue
documentation:
A queue of tuples. Each tuple is (func, args), 
where the function's first argument is of type `T` (generic),
and the functions other args are of "the correct type". Example:
```python3
def f(a: list, b: str, c: int):
    ...

q = mp.Queue()
args = ("foo", 33)
q.put((f, args))
```

methods.name: paragradio.v2025_02._internal.core.ParallelGR.put_cmd
documentation:
Put a command into the queue for the child
process to execute. Any extra args provided will be
passed to `f`.

methods.name: paragradio.v2025_02._internal.core.PGRWrapperCommon.start
documentation: Start the parallel process and its associated GUI.

class_variables.name: paragradio.v2025_02._internal.core.PGRWrapperCommon._pgr
documentation:
This handles interfacing with the parallel process. It 
shouldn't be used externally unless you know what you're doing.

methods.name: paragradio.v2025_02._internal.core.PGR_can_set_center_freq.set_center_freq
documentation: Set the center frequency of the SDR peripheral and any associated GUI elements.

methods.name: paragradio.v2025_02._internal.core.PGR_can_set_if_gain.set_if_gain
documentation: Set the Intermediate Frequency gain of the SDR peripheral.

methods.name: paragradio.v2025_02._internal.core.PGR_can_set_bb_gain.set_bb_gain
documentation: Set the Baseband gain of the SDR peripheral.

methods.name: paragradio.v2025_02._internal.core.PGR_can_set_samp_rate.set_samp_rate
documentation:
Set the sample rate. The specific meaning is
documented in the docstring of child classes.

methods.name: paragradio.v2025_02._internal.core.PGR_can_set_hw_filt_bw.set_hw_bb_filt
documentation:
Set the Hardware Baseband Filter SOMETHING TODO ABOUT CUTOFF FREQUENCY, which is proportional to the cutoff frequency of the baseband filter.

The HackRF One and many other SDR peripherals have a built-in filter that
precedes the Analog to Digital conversion. It is able to reduce or prevent
aliasing, which software filters cannot do.

Typically, you should set the baseband filter as low as possible for the signals that you wish to receive -- a tighter filter will more effectively reduce aliasing.

For more info, see the Hack RF One documentation about Sampling Rate and 
Baseband Filters <https://hackrf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/sampling_rate.html>.

methods.name: paragradio.v2025_02._internal.core.PGR_can_set_freq_offset.set_freq_offset
documentation:
Set the frequency offset.

When tuning the FM Radio, TODO FINISH EXPLANATION

methods.name: paragradio.v2025_02._internal.core.SpecAn.set_samp_rate
documentation:
Sets the sample rate of the SDR peripheral and the bandwidth
(the amount of viewable spectrum) of the GUI spectrum view.

Due to the physics of digital sampling, your sample rate is your bandwidth. 

To set the TODO, use `set_hw_filt_bw TODO fix`.

methods.name: paragradio.v2025_02._internal.core.SpecAnSim.__init__
documentation: Create a Paragradio Simulated Spectrum Analyzer with simulated activity on 93.5 MHz.

methods.name: paragradio.v2025_02._internal.core.SpecAnSim.set_center_freq
documentation: Set the center frequency of simulated spectrum view.

methods.name: paragradio.v2025_02._internal.core.WBFM_Rx.__init__
documentation: Create a Paragradio Wideband FM Receiver.

methods.name: paragradio.v2025_02._internal.core.PSK_Tx_loop.set_data
documentation: Update what data is being repeatedly transmitted.
